item.title: 我的故事 | STORY OF MY LIFE
item.author: 1995hash

item.title: 我的故事 | STORY OF MY LIFE
item.description:
卡伦佐2001

发表于 4 月 27 日

我的名字是 Benoit BIZIMANA 我出生于 2001 年 1 月 21 日，我已于 2022 年完成中学学习，并在 RUSUMO 高中学习物理、化学和生物学 (PCB)。 我对编码非常感兴趣，因为今天的世界是一个随着技术迅速发展的数字世界，这让我印象深刻，成为一名优秀的软件开发人员。 所以，我想让所有阅读这篇关于我的人生的简短调查的人

一旦暂停，karenzo2001 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，karenzo2001 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，karenzo2001 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 karenzo2001 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 karenzo2001 的方法：

降低 karenzo2001 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 karenzo2001 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Karenzo2001

Posted on Apr 27

My name is Benoit BIZIMANA I was born on 21 January 2001 I have completed my secondary studies in 2022 and I took Physics, Chemistry and Biology(PCB) at RUSUMO HIGH SCHOOL. I am very interested in coding due to, To day's world is a digital developing instantly with technology and this led me impressed to be a good software developer from to day. So, I would like to make every body who is reading this very short survey of my life

Once suspended, karenzo2001 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, karenzo2001 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by karenzo2001 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If karenzo2001 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag karenzo2001:

Make all posts by karenzo2001 less visible

karenzo2001 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging karenzo2001 will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: karenzo2001

item.title: 人工智能在营销中的未来：洞察和预测 | The Future of AI in Marketing: Insights and Predictions
item.author: kitomind

item.title: 如何在 Ruby 中加载代码 | How to Load Code in Ruby
item.description:
在 Ruby 中加载代码和访问文件相关常量的方法有很多种。 我们可以通过将关注点分离和处理到类中并仅引入我们依赖的类来创建清晰的体系结构。

许多全栈框架，如 Rails 和 Hanami，都提供了一种内置方法来访问我们想要的类，只要我们坚持一定的约定即可。 这是如何运作的？

在本文中，我们将探索加载代码的三种不同选项：使用load 、 require和autoload 。 我们还将研究 Ruby gem Zeitwerk 。 在我们深入研究 Ruby 中提供的代码加载选项之前，让我们快速浏览一下$LOAD_PATH以掌握代码加载的工作原理。

红宝石中的$LOAD_PATH

在我们深入探讨这个主题之前，让我们总结一下 Ruby 中的$LOAD_PATH ，因为它对于理解加载的工作原理至关重要。

全局变量$LOAD_PATH或更短的$:引用在数组中注册了 Ruby 源文件的所有目录。 因此，当您在bundle exec前面加上您的程序名称时，Ruby 会将所有程序 gem 添加到加载路径中。

然后，当我们加载或需要一个文件时，Ruby 遍历这个数组并搜索文件名。 这可能会显着增加启动过程的时间，因为我们可能必须在硬盘上的许多地方搜索给定文件。

牢记这一点，我们现在将详细探讨一些 Ruby 代码加载选项。

在 Ruby 中加载代码的选项

我们有三个主要的加载代码选项：

  • 使用load方法。 每次调用该方法时，都会加载并解析指定文件中的 Ruby 程序。
  • 有了这个，我们只加载和解析给定的文件一次。
  • 第三个选项是autoload 。 当我们使用一个尚不存在的常量时，我​​们预先声明 Ruby 应该需要一个指定的文件。

在 Ruby 中load一个文件

如前所述，通过load我们解析并执行 Ruby 文件。
如果我们设置为参数的文件名是相对路径，即以./或../开头，则文件将相对于当前工作目录加载。

请记住：这并不意味着它与加载所在的文件相关——而是与启动 Ruby 进程的文件相关。 如果您想知道工作目录是什么，请运行Dir.pwd 。 否则，Ruby 在$LOAD_PATH中搜索具有给定名称的文件。

让我们看看负载是如何工作的：

 # foo.rb $x = 4 # define global Variable class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end end

然后我们用load "foo.rb"加载文件。

当我们加载这个文件时会发生什么？ 全局变量$x被创建并设置为整数 4。常量Foo也被创建，我们可以从中实例化一个对象。 到目前为止，这看起来就像更熟悉的require调用。

但是当我们再次load这个文件时会发生什么？ 如果我们更改了全局值$x ，它将重置为 4。

在旧的 Ruby 版本中，我们也可能会收到一个警告，指出常量Foo正在被重新定义。

另一方面，在第一次运行期间， require只加载和解析文件一次。

用load包装文件

现在，每次我们加载或需要一个文件时，我们都会污染全局命名空间。 为避免这种情况，我们可以使用 wrap 参数设置为true load方法。 这将创建一个匿名模块，其中放置了加载文件中定义的所有常量和方法。 这是一个例子：

 # file to be loaded foo.rb class Foo puts self.name # => Here we will see where our Foo class is located in def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end end ############################### # Now in another file run this: load "foo.rb", true

如果你运行它，Ruby 将输出如下内容： #<Module:0x00007fa3430682e8>::Foo 。

为什么这有用？ 一方面，我们没有污染全局命名空间。 但是由于 Ruby 创建了一个匿名模块，我们只能通过一些技巧来访问我们加载的内容。

尽管如此，如果我们想在我们的应用程序中配置一些东西或设置一些东西，包装文件仍然有用。 这样，我们就可以实现我们想要的，而不会留下任何东西。

访问包装文件常量

有两种方法可以访问我们在包装文件中创建的常量。 第一个也是最明显的是将模块名称设置为 wrap 参数的参数。

 class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end end ############################### # Now in another file run this: module Parent end load "./code_loading/file.rb", Parent Parent::Foo.new

以下是我们如何访问匿名 Ruby 模块：

 class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end end throw :wrapper, Module.nesting.last ############################################ # When we load the file we catch the error # and make the module accessible mod = catch :wrapper do load "./code_loading/file.rb", true end mod::Foo.new

如果加载文件的作者做了这样的事情，它会破坏沙箱：

现在Foo在全局命名空间中，没有办法防止这种污染。

使用require加载 Ruby 代码

和load方法一样， require是Kernel模块中的一个方法。 这两种方法的最大区别在于它们在多次调用时的行为方式。

虽然load总是重新评估文件， require不会。 它的反应也是不同的。 如果您第一次调用require并且该方法实际加载了一些东西，它会返回true ，否则，它会返回false 。 另一方面， load总是返回true 。

如果我们需要一个简单的文件，还require在$LOAD_PATH中搜索。 当然，这意味着我们仍然有与load相同的缺点。

使用require_relative更简单的文件搜索

如果它不存在，我们会得到一个LoadError 。

不幸的是，截至目前， require没有像load那样的wrap参数，因此require总是污染全局命名空间。 但是最近有一些关于 Ruby 问题跟踪器的讨论，以向require方法添加wrap之类的东西。

我们在 Ruby 中加载代码的第三个选项是autoload 。 使用autoload ，我们预先告诉 Ruby VM 可以访问哪些常量以及在哪里找到和加载它们。

这种方法的一大优势是我们只加载我们真正需要的文件。 因此，启动过程可以非常快，因为我们只需为使用的东西付费。 当我们实际需要常量时， autoload使用require方法需要文件。

以下是我们如何设置自动加载：

 ### lib/services/foo.rb module Services class Foo def bar puts "bar" end end end ### lib/services.rb module Services autoload :Foo, "#{__dir__}/services/foo.rb" # Now we have access to the Foo constant in this module # and we will load the file when we actually need it. # Foo.new.bar => loads the file and prints "bar" end

用于管理加载文件的最流行的 gem 是Zeitwerk ，它与autoload一起使用。 现在让我们看看 Zeitwerk 是如何工作的以及如何设置它。

使用 Zeitwerk 加载代码

约定是每个新的子目录都是一个新模块，每个文件都定义一个与该文件同名的类。

它是如何工作的？ 这是一些非常非常简化的伪代码：

 module Zeitwerk class Loader def initialize() @root_dir = [] end def push_dir(dir) @root_dirs << dir end def setup # scan root dirs and define autoloads for each file @root_dirs.each do |dir| Dir.foreach(dir) do |file| next if file == "." || file == ".." autoload file.class_name.to_sym, File.expand_path(file, dir) end end end end end loader = Zeitwerk::Loader.new loader.push_dir(__dir__) loader.setup

现在，通过最后一次调用loader.setup ，我们可以访问其中一个文件中相对于当前文件定义的每个常量。
当然，这段简化的代码并没有突出显示Zeitwerk具有的许多其他强大功能，例如预先加载和处理命名空间。 这超出了本文的范围，我将留给您更深入地挖掘和探索Zeitwerk必须提供的所有功能。

如何使用 Roda 设置Zeitwerk

由于 Rails 和 Hanami 默认使用Zeitwerk ，让我们看看如何为 Roda 配置Zeitwerk ：

 ### in config.ru loader = Zeitwerk::Loader.new loader.push_dir(__dir__) loader.push_dir("#{__dir__}/lib") if ENV['RACK_ENV'] == 'production' loader.setup Zeitwerk::Loader.eager_load_all run App else loader.enable_reloading loader.setup run ->(env) { loader.reload App.call(env) } end

如果我们这样配置我们的应用程序，我们会在开发过程中重新加载代码，在生产过程中进行预加载。 这样，开发期间的启动时间很快，因为只加载立即需要的文件。 在生产中，我们预先加载所有内容并有更快的响应时间。

包起来

在本文中，我们首先快速浏览了 Ruby 中的$LOAD_PATH以奠定代码加载工作原理的基础。 然后，我们详细探讨了加载代码的三个选项： load 、 require和autoload （与Zeitwerk gem 配合得很好）。

那么，我们应该如何加载我们的代码呢？ 大多数时候，我们应该使用require和Zeitwerk来自动化。 如果您有特殊要求并希望将加载的代码放入命名空间，带有 wrap 参数的load方法是一个不错的选择。

快乐的代码加载！

PS If you'd like to read Ruby Magic posts as soon as they get off the press, subscribe to our Ruby Magic newsletter and never miss a single post ! PS 如果您想在 Ruby Magic 发布后立即阅读它们，请订阅我们的 Ruby Magic 时事通讯，不要错过任何一篇文章！



原文:

There are many ways to load code and access file-related constants in Ruby. We can create a clear architecture by separating and handling concerns into classes and pulling in only the classes we depend on.

Many full-stack frameworks like Rails and Hanami offer a built-in method to access the classes we want, as long as we stick with a certain convention. How does this work?

In this post, we will explore three different options for loading code: using load, require, and autoload. We will also look into the Ruby gem Zeitwerk. Zeitwerk is the default code-loading mechanism for many new projects, and a lot of established projects are switching to this great gem. Rails and Hanami both utilize Zeitwerk as their code-loading tool.

Before we dive into the code loading options on offer in Ruby, let's take a quick look at $LOAD_PATH to get to grips with how code loading works.

$LOAD_PATH in Ruby

Before we dive deeper into this topic, let's summarize the $LOAD_PATH in Ruby, as it is essential in understanding how loading works.

The global variable $LOAD_PATH or shorter $: references all directories with Ruby source files registered in an array. So when you prepend bundle exec with your program name, Ruby adds all the program gems to the load path.

Then, when we load or require a file, Ruby iterates over this array and searches for the file name. This can add significant time to the booting process, as we might have to search for a given file in many places on the hard disk.

Keeping that in mind, we'll now explore some Ruby code-loading options in detail.

Options for Loading Code in Ruby

We have three main options for loading code:

  • Using the load method. This loads and parses a Ruby program in a specified file every time you call the method.
  • The require method. With this, we load and parse a given file only once.
  • The third option is autoload. We declare upfront that Ruby should require a specified file when we use a constant that doesn't exist yet.

load a File in Ruby

As mentioned, with load we parse and execute a Ruby file.
If the filename we set as an argument is a relative path, i.e., it starts with ./ or ../, the file will be loaded relative to the current working directory.

Remember: This doesn't mean it's relative to the file where the load is located — it's instead relative to the file that started the Ruby process. If you want to know what the working directory is, run Dir.pwd. Otherwise, Ruby searches for a file with the given name in the $LOAD_PATH.

Let's see how load works:

# foo.rb
$x = 4 # define global Variable class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end
end

Then we load the file with load "foo.rb".

What happens when we load this file? The global variable $x is created and set to the integer 4. The constant Foo is also created, and we can instantiate an object from it. So far, this seems just like the more familiar require call.

But what happens when we load this file again? If we have changed our global value $x, it resets to 4.

In older Ruby versions, we also might get a warning that the constant Foo is being redefined.

Another problem with load is that every reload takes some time to run as the Ruby VM is eval-ing the file. require, on the other hand, loads and parses a file just once, during the first run.

Wrapping the File with load

Right now, every time we load or require a file, we pollute the global namespace. To avoid this, we can use the load method with the wrap parameter set to true. This will create an anonymous module where all the constants and methods defined in a loaded file are placed. Here is an example:

# file to be loaded foo.rb class Foo puts self.name # => Here we will see where our Foo class is located in def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end
end ###############################
# Now in another file run this: load "foo.rb", true

If you run this, Ruby will output something like this: #<Module:0x00007fa3430682e8>::Foo.

Why is this useful? For one thing, we are not polluting the global namespace. But since Ruby creates an anonymous module, we can only access the stuff we load with some trickery.

Nevertheless, wrapping the file is still useful if we want to configure something in our application or set some things up. This way, we can achieve what we want without leaving anything behind.

Accessing the Wrapped File Constants

There are two ways to access the constants we create in the wrapped file. The first and most obvious is to set a module name as the argument for the wrap parameter.

class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end
end ###############################
# Now in another file run this: module Parent
end
load "./code_loading/file.rb", Parent Parent::Foo.new

Here is how we can have access to the anonymous Ruby module:

class Foo def bar puts "I'm doing it now" end
end throw :wrapper, Module.nesting.last ############################################
# When we load the file we catch the error
# and make the module accessible mod = catch :wrapper do load "./code_loading/file.rb", true
end mod::Foo.new

There is a hidden problem with wrap. If the author of the loaded file does something like this, it will break the sandbox:

Now Foo is in the global namespace, and there is no way to prevent this pollution.

Ruby Code Loading with require

Just like the load method, require is a method in the Kernel module. The big difference between these two methods is how they behave on multiple calls.

While load always reevaluates a file, require doesn't. Its response is also different. If you call require for the first time and the method actually loads something, it returns true, otherwise, it returns false. load, on the other hand, always returns true.

Some pseudo-code for require would look something like this:

def require(file_path) if !already_loaded_files? file_path eval(File.read(find_file_in_load_path(file_path))) mark_file_as_loaded! file_path true else false end
end

require accepts absolute paths for files as well as just a simple name. If we need a simple file, require also searches in the $LOAD_PATH. This, of course, means that we still have the same disadvantages we had with load.

Simpler File Searching with require_relative

require_relative always looks for a file that is relative to the current file you are working on. require_relative doesn't iterate over a directory, and no real search is involved. Ruby looks into the path specified and tries to load the file. If it isn't there, we get a LoadError.

Unfortunately, as of now, require doesn't have a wrap parameter like load does, so require always pollutes the global namespace. But there has been some recent discussion on the Ruby issue tracker to add something like wrap to the require method.

autoload Code Loading in Ruby

Our third option for code loading in Ruby is autoload. With autoload, we tell the Ruby VM upfront what constants might be accessed and where to find and load them.

A big advantage of this approach is that we only load the files we really need. Therefore, the booting process can be really fast as we just pay for what we use. When we actually need the constant, autoload requires the file with the method require.

Here's how we set up an autoload:

### lib/services/foo.rb
module Services class Foo def bar puts "bar" end end
end ### lib/services.rb
module Services autoload :Foo, "#{__dir__}/services/foo.rb" # Now we have access to the Foo constant in this module # and we will load the file when we actually need it. # Foo.new.bar => loads the file and prints "bar"
end

The most popular gem for managing loading files is Zeitwerk, and it works with autoload. Now let's see how Zeitwerk works and how to set it up.

Code Loading with Zeitwerk

Zeitwerk takes a directory and makes every file underneath it available to load. The convention is that every new sub-directory is a new module, and every file defines a class with the same name as the file.

Zeitwerk offers an option for code reloading that is useful during development. Rails uses this mode in an active development environment.

How does it work under the hood? Here is some very, very simplified pseudo-code:

module Zeitwerk class Loader def initialize() @root_dir = [] end def push_dir(dir) @root_dirs << dir end def setup # scan root dirs and define autoloads for each file @root_dirs.each do |dir| Dir.foreach(dir) do |file| next if file == "." || file == ".." autoload file.class_name.to_sym, File.expand_path(file, dir) end end end end
end loader = Zeitwerk::Loader.new
loader.push_dir(__dir__)
loader.setup

Now, with the last call to loader.setup, we get access to every constant defined in one of the files relative to the current file.
Of course, this simplified code doesn't highlight many of the other great features that Zeitwerk has, like eager loading and handling namespaces. That's outside of the scope of this post, and I'll leave it to you to dig deeper and explore all Zeitwerk has to offer.

How to Set Up Zeitwerk with Roda

Since Rails and Hanami use Zeitwerk as their default, let's see how we can configure Zeitwerk for Roda:

### in config.ru loader = Zeitwerk::Loader.new
loader.push_dir(__dir__)
loader.push_dir("#{__dir__}/lib") if ENV['RACK_ENV'] == 'production' loader.setup Zeitwerk::Loader.eager_load_all run App
else loader.enable_reloading loader.setup run ->(env) { loader.reload App.call(env) }
end

If we configure our app like this, we have code reloading during development and eager loading in production. This way, the boot time during development is fast, as only the files that are immediately needed are loaded. In production, we load everything upfront and have a faster response time.

Wrapping Up

In this post, we first took a quick look at $LOAD_PATH in Ruby to lay the foundations of how code loading works. We then explored three options to load code in some detail: load, require, and autoload (which works well with the Zeitwerk gem).

So, how should we load our code? Most of the time, we should use require and Zeitwerk to automate that. If you have special requirements and want to put loaded code into a namespace, the load method with a wrap parameter is a good option.

Happy code loading!

P.S. If you'd like to read Ruby Magic posts as soon as they get off the press, subscribe to our Ruby Magic newsletter and never miss a single post!
item.author: choallin

item.title: 如何在 DigitalOcean 上创建 Kubernetes 集群 | How to Create a Kubernetes Cluster on DigitalOcean
item.description:
翻转

发表于 4 月 27 日

创建 Kubernetes 集群：

  • 从控制面板的创建菜单中，单击 Kubernetes。
  • 选择 Kubernetes 版本。 默认情况下会选择最新版本，如果您对早期版本没有特定需求，则这是最佳选择。
  • 选择数据中心区域。
  • 自定义默认节点池，选择节点池名称，并添加其他节点池。
  • 为集群命名，选择您希望集群所属的项目，并可选择添加标签。 您选择的任何标签都将应用于集群及其工作节点。
  • 单击创建集群。 配置集群需要几分钟时间。

验证集群是否已创建

通过单击操作下载集群配置文件，然后从集群主页下载配置。

运行以下 kubectl 命令来检查节点：

kubectl –kubeconfig={CONFIG FILE PATH} get nodes

您应该获得如下图所示的节点：

在 Kubernetes 上部署应用程序

在这之后，您就有了一个正在运行的 Kubernetes 集群，是时候使用它了！

现在是时候在 Kubernetes 集群上部署应用程序了。 作为建议，您可以使用 Ant Media Server 一个超低延迟的 WebRTC 实时流媒体引擎。

只需按照此处的文档操作

一旦暂停，flipsnap 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，flipsnap 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，flipsnap 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 flipsnap 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 flipsnap 的方法：

通过 flipsnap 降低所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 flipsnap 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

flipsnap

Posted on Apr 27

To create a Kubernetes cluster:

  • From the Create menu in the control panel, click Kubernetes.
  • Select a Kubernetes version. The latest version is selected by default and is the best choice if you have no specific need for an earlier version.
  • Choose a data center region.
  • Customize the default node pool, choose the node pool names, and add additional node pools.
  • Name the cluster, select the project you want the cluster to belong to, and optionally add a tag. Any tags you choose will be applied to the cluster and its worker nodes.
  • Click Create Cluster. Provisioning the cluster takes several minutes.

Verify the Cluster was created

Download the cluster configuration file by clicking Actions, then Download the Config from the cluster home page.

run the following kubectl command to check nodes:

kubectl –kubeconfig={CONFIG FILE PATH} get nodes

You should get nodes as in the following image:

Deploying An Application on Kubernetes

After this point, you have a running Kubernetes Cluster and it is time to use it!

Now it's time to deploy an application on the Kubernetes cluster. As a suggestion, you can use Ant Media Server an Ultra-low latency WebRTC live streaming engine.

Simply follow the documentation here

Once suspended, flipsnap will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, flipsnap will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by flipsnap will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If flipsnap is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag flipsnap:

Make all posts by flipsnap less visible

flipsnap consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging flipsnap will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: flipsnap

item.title: MAHAMA 营地生计 | MAHAMA CAMP LIVELIHOOD
item.description:
发表于 4 月 27 日

马哈马营地的生计是马哈马营地难民的主要关注点 通过在营地内参加各种活动，设法创造了一些表面上的生计

一旦被暂停，aime1991 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，aime1991 将可以再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aime1991 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aime1991 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 aime1991 的方法：

降低 aime1991 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 aime1991 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

AIME1991

Posted on Apr 27

livelihood in mahama camp is majorconcern for refugees in mahama camp have managed to create some semblance of livelihhod by engaging in various activities whithin the camp

faisalcreativeheads - Apr 27

Once suspended, aime1991 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aime1991 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aime1991 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aime1991 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag aime1991:

Make all posts by aime1991 less visible

aime1991 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging aime1991 will restore default visibility to their posts.
